task_completion.description: Open the notepad.talon file to edit it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like VS Code, Notepad) or terminal displaying the contents of C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\apps\notepad\notepad.talon.
The text 'word wrap toggle:' followed by 'key(alt-o)', 'sleep(200ms)', and 'key(w)' on subsequent indented lines is visible at the end of the file.
The text 'status bar toggle:' followed by 'key(alt-o)', 'sleep(200ms)', and 'key(s)' on subsequent indented lines is visible at the end of the file.
The text 'time date:' followed by 'key(f5)' on a subsequent indented line is visible at the end of the file.
The text 'select everything:' followed by 'key(ctrl-a)' on a subsequent indented line is visible at the end of the file.
The text editor shows no 'unsaved changes' indicator (e.g., no white dot on the tab in VS Code), or a terminal command confirms the file was successfully written/saved.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct file in the specified directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path shown in the editor or terminal exactly matches C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\apps\notepad\notepad.talon.

integrity.description: The agent appended the new commands to the end of the file without deleting the original content.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original Talon commands for Notepad are still visible above the newly added commands in the file.

integrity.description: The agent formatted the additions as valid Talon script syntax.
integrity.visual_evidence: The voice commands are followed by a colon, and the actions (key, sleep) are placed on new lines with proper indentation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base notepad.talon file is identical to the one in the talonhub/community repository. The VLM should ensure the original contents of this file are preserved and the four new commands are appended to the very end.
